classes.name: Oral NSAIDs
classes.description: Oral nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (naproxen, ibuprofen, meloxicam, celecoxib, diclofenac, etc.) — first-line therapy for musculoskeletal pain and inflammation.
classes.plainDescription: A pill that reduces pain, swelling, and inflammation — commonly used for joint and muscle pain.
classes.indication: Musculoskeletal pain & inflammation (e.g. osteoarthritis, back pain)

references.label: Illinois Medicaid Preferred Drug List — Analgesics/NSAIDs section, effective July 1, 2026

glossary.definition: Insurer sign-off needed before the drug is covered.
Must fail a preferred drug first before the plan covers this one.

preferredAgent.sig: 1 tablet (400 mg) by mouth every 4–6 hours as needed for pain; for osteoarthritis/rheumatoid arthritis, 1200–3200 mg daily in divided doses
preferredAgent.plainSig: Take one tablet by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed for pain, or as directed for arthritis. Do not exceed 3200 mg in 24 hours.

alternatives.drug: Naproxen (tablet, suspension, delayed-release tablet, sodium tablet)
alternatives.note: Preferred (no PA) on the IL HFS PDL alongside ibuprofen.

records.tier: Preferred Drug List — preferred generic, no PA required
records.verificationNote: Re-read the full HFS PDL PDF (effective July 1, 2026, 150 pages) directly from illinois.gov, pages 4-5, 'Analgesics - Anti-Inflammatory: Nonsteroidal Anti-Inflammatory Agents (NSAIDs)' table, line by line, after a PR review (codex bot) correctly flagged that an earlier pass had misclassified Etodolac, Flurbiprofen tablet, and Ketoprofen capsule as PA-required when the PDL lists all three PREFERRED. This pass transcribed every PREFERRED/NON_PREFERRED line item verbatim rather than grouping by drug family, which is what caused the original error. Marked partial, not verified: the PDL does not state a class-specific PA sentence for NSAIDs (unlike ADHD/PPI/antipsychotic classes, which have explicit footnotes) — 'non-preferred requires PA' reflects standard HFS PDL convention rather than a quoted sentence for this specific class, so the PA determination itself is inferred, not read directly. Illinois FFS Medicaid pharmacy is administered directly by HFS (no contracted PBM). Sig for ibuprofen 400mg tablet is standard adult dosing from the DailyMed FDA label; the PDL itself lists coverage status only, not dosing.
